summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of AURA Tickets MVP. 29/30 backend pytest cases passing. Frontend flows verified end-to-end: landing, browse, event detail (tier + seatmap), login, signup, booking → checkout (with countdown timer + Pay with Stripe button), profile (bookings list), organizer dashboard (analytics + events table), admin panel (approve/reject/feature). One real backend bug found: GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} returns 500 instead of handling Stripe CheckoutError gracefully.

critical.issue: Endpoint returns 500 Internal Server Error when emergentintegrations StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status raises CheckoutError (e.g., 'No such checkout.session' under sk_test_emergent). Exception is not caught — propagates as 500. The CheckoutSuccess page (frontend) polls this endpoint, so any error from Stripe (incl. transient ones) will crash the success page. Wrap the get_checkout_status call in try/except and return last known status (or 502 with structured payload) instead of raising. Log: 'emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.CheckoutError: Failed to retrieve session status: ... No such checkout.session: cs_test_xxx'.

issues: Repeated 401 errors logged to browser console from /api/auth/me when unauthenticated visitor browses landing/events — functional but noisy. Suppress 401 console log or use HEAD-style probe.

action_items: Wrap StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status() in try/except CheckoutError in /api/checkout/status/{session_id} — return the last-known status from DB rather than raising 500. This unblocks the CheckoutSuccess page in test mode and is also a resiliency fix for prod.
(Optional) Silence console-level 401 warnings on /api/auth/me when no token is present (e.g., short-circuit in lib/auth.jsx checkAuth before calling axios).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~1070 lines — getting close to the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/events.py, routers/bookings.py, routers/payments.py, routers/admin.py.

Seat-hold concurrency is read-then-write (non-atomic). Under true concurrency two requests may both pass the availability check before either inserts. The test_two_holds_same_seat test confirmed it can race (it was lenient and accepted both 200s). Use a unique compound index on (event_id, seat) in a seats collection, or use findOneAndUpdate with $addToSet guarded by $not/$elemMatch to make seat reservation atomic.
create_access_token uses datetime objects in payload — pyjwt handles this, OK. But consider adding 'iat' claim for token freshness checks.

POST /auth/register and /auth/login set httponly+secure+samesite=none cookies AND return the token in JSON. Frontend stores JSON token in localStorage. This dual-channel is intentional but be aware: logout only deletes cookies, so localStorage token remains valid until JWT exp. Consider also clearing it client-side (looks like AuthProvider does, just confirming).

Demo seed creates 8 events but on every restart the existing collection blocks re-seed (good). However seed_demo does not idempotently update admin password if ADMIN_PASSWORD changes in .env — fine for MVP, flag for prod.
test_credentials: admin@aura.events/admin123, organizer@aura.events/organizer123, attendee@aura.events/attendee123 — all seeded and verified working
seed_data_creation: Backend startup seeds 3 users + 8 events automatically. No manual seed needed. Tests create transient TEST_-prefixed users/events; these are not cleaned up but are isolated.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_aura_backend.py runs in ~7s. The only failure is /api/checkout/status/{sid} 500 — once that's wrapped in try/except CheckoutError, all 30 should pass. Frontend works end-to-end. Stripe checkout completion cannot be tested via curl (real browser/Stripe flow required); the 500 is on the polling endpoint, not session creation which works.
rca of the issue: RCA for /api/checkout/status/{session_id} 500: In server.py checkout_status() calls `stripe.get_checkout_status(session_id)` from emergentintegrations. With sk_test_emergent test key, calling get_checkout_status on a session that doesn't exist in real Stripe raises emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.CheckoutError. This exception is not caught, so FastAPI returns 500. Reproduction: 1) POST /api/checkout/session with a valid booking — returns {url, session_id}. 2) GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} immediately after — returns 500 with 'No such checkout.session: cs_test_...'. Mitigation: wrap the get_checkout_status call in try/except CheckoutError and on failure return {status: tx['status'], payment_status: tx['payment_status'], booking_id: tx['booking_id']} (i.e., last-known DB state). This makes polling resilient to Stripe API errors and makes the CheckoutSuccess page work in dev/test.
